npc_dialogue_rumor: {npc} lowers their voice and repeats a rumor worth testing: {rumor}
{npc} treats it as common gossip, but the implication is sharp: {rumor}
{npc} insists the town is pretending not to notice this: {rumor}
npc_dialogue_warning: {npc} offers a warning: whatever stalks {wilderness} is tied to {threat}.
{npc} warns you not to trust the easy path toward {dungeon}; {threat} has made it too quiet.
{npc} says the safest mistake is still safer than ignoring signs around {wilderness}.
npc_dialogue_request: {npc} asks for capable help before {problem} turns the whole district against itself.
{npc} wants proof, not heroics, and asks you to bring back something that confirms the truth.
{npc} asks you to look into a matter quietly before the council hears the wrong version.
npc_dialogue_secret: {npc} hints that one secret matters more than any oath in town: {secret}.
{npc} admits there is a private truth behind the public trouble: {secret}.
{npc} only says enough to make the silence dangerous: {secret}.
npc_dialogue_local_trouble: {npc} ties today's trouble directly to the settlement's fear: {problem}.
{npc} says every argument in {settlement} eventually circles back to this: {problem}.
{npc} thinks the town is misreading the first sign of a bigger problem: {problem}.
npc_dialogue_faction_hint: {npc} points toward a quiet faction struggle hiding behind ordinary business.
{npc} suggests someone powerful benefits whenever talk turns away from {artifact}.
{npc} hints that the people profiting from {problem} are not the people being blamed.
npc_dialogue_dungeon_clue: {npc} believes the best clue lies inside {dungeon}, not in any town testimony.
{npc} says {dungeon} keeps answering the wrong question too clearly to ignore.
{npc} thinks the route to understanding starts with what was hidden in {dungeon}.
npc_dialogue_wilderness_clue: {npc} says the real trail starts in {wilderness}, where even the harmless signs were arranged.
{npc} insists the wilderness clue matters because it points to intent, not just danger.
{npc} says anyone watching {wilderness} closely would notice the pattern sooner or later.
npc_dialogue_trade_lead: {npc} mentions a trade lead: someone is paying too well for ordinary goods and asking no safe questions.
{npc} says {service} is suddenly harder to find because someone upstream is redirecting supplies.
{npc} thinks a market exchange may reveal more than a shrine confession.
npc_dialogue_personal_oddity: {npc} shares a personal oddity that may matter later: {appearance}.
{npc} reveals a habit that others dismiss but you should remember: {fear}.
{npc} offers a detail too specific to be meaningless: {personality}.
encounter_avoid_success: You avoid {threat} by following the safer pattern: {avoidance}
You slip past {threat} without proving you were ever there. {avoidance}
You deny {threat} a clean sighting and keep moving. {avoidance}
encounter_approach_success: You negotiate a tense distance with {threat}. {negotiate} Reaction: {reaction}
You approach openly enough to be understood and cautiously enough to survive. {negotiate}
You make contact without drawing steel, and {threat} gives you a moment to speak. {negotiate}
encounter_approach_trade: A trade settles the moment for now: {reward} changes hands and both sides step back.
You trade information and caution instead of blows; {threat} values the exchange more than pride.
The encounter turns into a hard bargain, and {reward} becomes the price of safer passage.
encounter_approach_help: You offer practical help rather than dominance, and {threat} pauses long enough to accept it.
Aid buys a narrow peace: helping with {intent} shifts the tone of the encounter.
You solve one immediate problem for {threat}, which is enough to prevent escalation.
encounter_investigate_success: Careful investigation reveals {reward}. Preparation that helps: {preparation}
You study the scene instead of rushing it and uncover {reward}.
Your caution pays off; the signs resolve into a usable clue: {reward}
encounter_investigate_follow: You follow the signs farther than comfort allows and learn where the trail really wants you to go.
Investigation turns into a pursuit of meaning, and the trail now points beyond the first clue.
You follow the disturbance instead of the obvious track, and the pattern becomes clearer.
encounter_failed_forward: The contact goes badly, but not uselessly; you are forced back with one hard-won lead.
You lose control of the moment, yet still carry away something worth following.
The encounter turns against you, but the retreat leaves behind a clue you can still use.
encounter_costly_success: You come away with a success that still costs you breathing room and supplies.
The problem yields, but only after exacting a practical price.
You succeed narrowly enough that the cost will matter later.
encounter_strange_omen: A strange omen lingers after the encounter, as if something else was observing both sides.
The scene leaves behind an omen too deliberate to dismiss.
You leave with the sense that the encounter was only the surface of a stranger pattern.
search_discovery: The search also reveals a detail someone hoped would stay ordinary.
You notice evidence of intent, not just disorder.
What you find matters because it suggests purpose behind the scene.
inspect_discovery: Close study turns suspicion into a workable lead.
The place gives up one detail too deliberate to ignore.
Inspection reveals the mark of planning rather than accident.
town_explore_discovery: The streets do not hide their mood well; people are already adjusting to the trouble.
Something about the place feels rehearsed, as if the locals expect the wrong question.
You get the sense that the town knows more than it can safely say aloud.
wilderness_explore_discovery: Even without direct contact, the land feels recently interpreted by something intelligent.
The route teaches you more about caution than speed.
You find no immediate confrontation, but the silence is organized rather than empty.
retreat_consequences: The withdrawal preserves your options, but it also concedes the next move.
Retreat buys time at the cost of initiative.
You leave alive and observant, which is not the same thing as leaving unchanged.
check_critical_failure_additions: The mistake teaches the environment how to punish you next.
You confirm the danger's advantage more clearly than your own.
The failure leaves the situation more exposed and less forgiving.
check_failure_additions: You do not leave empty-handed, but what you learn is mostly about resistance.
The attempt fails in a way that narrows later options.
You understand the obstacle better, though not on favorable terms.
check_partial_success_additions: The gain is real, but so is the immediate cost.
You get enough to proceed, not enough to feel safe.
Progress comes with a price that cannot be mistaken for victory.
check_success_additions: The result gives you a cleaner next question to ask.
You gain clarity without pretending the danger is gone.
What you learn now should matter in the next difficult choice.
check_critical_success_additions: You also seize an advantage that should survive the moment.
The result shifts the balance of initiative in your favor.
You gain both clarity and leverage for what comes next.
